---
title: "Science"
description: "Research and development and the research workforce"
---

Research, and the knowledge it produces, is an engine of a modern economy and
society — a country's long-run prosperity rests heavily on what it discovers and
learns to do. These charts cover overall research and development spending and the
people who carry that research out. Business-led R&D has its own page:
[Innovation](../innovation/index.qmd).

```{python}
#| echo: false
import os
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from pipeline.config import (
    CANADA_COLOR, PEER_COLOR, HIGHLIGHT_WIDTH, PEER_WIDTH,
    HIGHLIGHT_COUNTRY, PEER_COUNTRIES, get_data_date,
)
from pipeline.charts import peer_comparison_line, ranked_bar
```

## Where Canada stands

A scorecard of where Canada ranks among the 17 peers on each measure (latest available year). Dots are ordered by rank, so **further right is more favourable for Canada** (lower-is-better measures are flipped accordingly). Canada is red, key comparators in colour; hover a dot for its value and rank.

```{python}
#| echo: false
from pipeline.charts import page_snapshot
page_snapshot("science").show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
```

## R&D expenditure (% of GDP)

Gross Domestic Expenditure on R&D (GERD) as a share of GDP — the standard
measure of how much a country invests in research and development across
government, business, and academia.

::: {.callout-note}
**Data lag:** R&D statistics are reported to the OECD with a delay of typically
2–3 years, and countries report at different speeds, so the most recent year may
not yet have full coverage.
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
rd_path = "data/science/oecd_rd_expenditure.csv"
if os.path.exists(rd_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(rd_path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(df, "year", "rd_pct_gdp",
        title="", yaxis_title="R&D expenditure (% of GDP)", show_average=True,
        initial_visible=["USA"])
    fig.update_layout(annotations=[dict(
        text=f"Source: OECD MSTI | Data as of: {get_data_date(rd_path)}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.34,
        showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("R&D data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Latest R&D spending: ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(rd_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(rd_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "rd_pct_gdp", "R&D expenditure (% of GDP)",
        f"Source: OECD MSTI | Data as of: {get_data_date(rd_path)}",
        min_countries=15, hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.2f}%<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## Researchers per 1,000 employed

The number of researchers (full-time equivalent) per 1,000 people employed — a
measure of how much of the workforce is engaged in research.

```{python}
#| echo: false
res_path = "data/science/oecd_researchers.csv"
if os.path.exists(res_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(res_path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(df, "year", "researchers_per_1000",
        title="", yaxis_title="Researchers per 1,000 employed", show_average=True)
    fig.update_layout(annotations=[dict(
        text=f"Source: OECD MSTI | Data as of: {get_data_date(res_path)}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.34,
        showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Researcher data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Latest researchers per 1,000: ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(res_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(res_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "researchers_per_1000", "Researchers per 1,000 employed",
        f"Source: OECD MSTI | Data as of: {get_data_date(res_path)}",
        min_countries=12, hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.1f}<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```


::: {.callout-note}
Comparable cross-country measures of educational attainment and PISA test scores are not charted here: the underlying OECD series mix different national survey methodologies, so they are not directly comparable without careful harmonisation.
:::

## Download data

- [R&D Expenditure, GERD (CSV)](../data/science/oecd_rd_expenditure.csv) — OECD MSTI
- [Researchers per 1,000 Employed (CSV)](../data/science/oecd_researchers.csv) — OECD MSTI
